Dear Mr. Kokanour:
Thank you for your letter requesting information relevant to
the filing obligations of a political action committee.
In your letter you ask for exact filing dates.
The filing
dates will vary depending on the type of election.
Please
refer to pages 3, 4 and s of the committee Reporting Guidelines,
a copy of which is enclosed.
These pages outline the filing
periods.
also, your local election official will he ehle to
provide you with exact dates in connection with any election.
You also ask if it is necessary to fern a committee hefore any
expenditures are made.
The definition of committee can he
found on page 22 of your "Handbook for Election officials“ or
on page i of the Committee Reporting Guidelines.
The key to
oomittee status is when a group raised or spends ssno or more
in a calendar year for political purposes.
When the $500 level
has seen reached. the group will then have ten days in which to
file a Statement of organisation. Fem 410, with the Secretary of
state's office.
The Secretary of state will issue the group a
committee identification number which must he used when filing
campaign statements.
The shove describes Type A committees or
what is more commonly referred to as Recipient comittees.
The
Recipient comuittee is the only committee which is required to
register (file a Form an) and it is the only committee uiat
does not terminate automatically at the and of each calendar
year.
a Recipient committee is terminated by filing a
Termination Statement, Form 415, stating that the committee has
fulfilled all the necessary obligations prior to termination.

a fact sheet which outlines the conditions under which
a comictee may terminate.

sections.question: What is an exact filing date? Does a committee need to be formed before there are any expenditures? What else needs to be done to get off to a correct start?
sections.conclusion: The filing dates vary depending on the type of election and should be found in the Committee Reporting Guidelines (pages 3-5). A group must reach the $500 threshold in a calendar year for political purposes before committee status is triggered, at which point they have ten days to file a Statement of Organization (Form 410). Recipient committees are the only type required to register and do not terminate automatically. Expenditure committees may also be formed if a group contributes $500 or more in nonmonetary contributions within a calendar year.

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks about filing date requirements for a political action committee, whether a committee must be formed before making expenditures, and what steps are necessary to properly establish a committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that committee formation is triggered when a group raises or spends $500 or more in a calendar year for political purposes, at which point they must file Form 410 within ten days. Filing dates vary by election type and should be obtained from pages 3-5 of the Committee Reporting Guidelines or from local election officials. Only Recipient committees are required to register, though Expenditure committees may also apply depending on the type of contributions made.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR quality is poor with numerous transcription errors. The document includes both the incoming request (pages 2-3) and FPPC's response (pages 1-2). Some dates and names are garbled but core legal information is extractable. Document ID and year provided in parsing notes (77ADV-77-188, 1977).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is an exact filing date? Does a committee need to be formed before there are any expenditures? What else needs to be done to get off to a correct start?

CONCLUSION: The filing dates vary depending on the type of election and should be found in the Committee Reporting Guidelines (pages 3-5). A group must reach the $500 threshold in a calendar year for political purposes before committee status is triggered, at which point they have ten days to file a Statement of Organization (Form 410). Recipient committees are the only type required to register and do not terminate automatically. Expenditure committees may also be formed if a group contributes $500 or more in nonmonetary contributions within a calendar year.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to inquiries about political action committee filing obligations, providing guidance on filing dates, committee formation thresholds, and the distinction between different committee types (Recipient vs. Expenditure committees).
